text: The Role of Minimally Invasive Surgery for Management of Small Bowel Obstruction Small bowel obstruction is one of the common surgical emergency conditions. The video demonstrate the feasibility and safety of laparoscopic approach for the treatment of small bowel obstruction. The first case was an eighty-seven year old male presented with abdominal pain and vomiting. CT scan show dilatation of jejunum with abrupt change in caliber at proximal ileum.

The surgeon gains safe access to the abdomen using a visualized trocar port, revealing the peritoneum upon entry. During diagnostic laparoscopy, a single adhesion band from the transverse colon to the mesentery is identified as the cause of proximal ileum obstruction. Sharp dissection with scissors is performed to lyse the adhesion, with caution advised regarding heat effects on the bowel.
The surgeon inspects the entire intestine, visually examining from the duodenal junction to the ileocecal valve, ensuring no remaining obstructions or abnormalities.
